have h1 : ∫ t in 0..x, t * exp t = (x - 1) * exp x + 1 := by
    have h2 : ∀ x : ℝ, ∫ t in 0..x, t * exp t = (x - 1) * exp x + 1 := by
      intro x
      have h3 : ∫ t in 0..x, t * exp t = (x - 1) * exp x + 1 := by
        simp [intervalIntegral.integral_const, mul_comm]
        <;> ring_nf <;> simp [exp_add, mul_add, add_mul]
        <;> ring
      exact h3
    specialize h2 x
    simpa using h2
  exact h1

have h1 : ∀ t : ℝ, HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => t * exp t) (fun t : ℝ => exp t + t * exp t) t := by
    intro t
    have h2 : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => t * exp t) (fun t : ℝ => exp t + t * exp t) t := by
      apply HasDerivAt.mul
      · -- Show that t has derivative 1 at t
        apply hasDerivAt_id
      · -- Show that exp t has derivative exp t at t
        apply hasDerivAt_exp
    exact h2
  have h2 : ∫ t in 0..x, t * exp t = (x - 1) * exp x + 1 := by
    have h3 : ∀ t : ℝ, HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => (t - 1) * exp t) (fun t : ℝ => exp t + (t - 1) * exp t) t := by
      intro t
      have h4 : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => (t - 1) * exp t) (fun t : ℝ => exp t + (t - 1) * exp t) t := by
        apply HasDerivAt.mul
        · -- Show that t - 1 has derivative 1 at t
          apply hasDerivAt_id
        · -- Show that exp t has derivative exp t at t
          apply hasDerivAt_exp
      exact h4
    have h5 : ∫ t in 0..x, t * exp t = ((x - 1) * exp x) - ((0 - 1) * exp (0 : ℝ)) := by
      apply intervalIntegral.integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt
      intro t ht
      have h4 : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => (t - 1) * exp t) (fun t : ℝ => exp t + (t - 1) * exp t) t := h3 t
      simpa using h4
    rw [h5]
    all_goals
      simp [exp_zero]
      <;> ring
  exact h2

To compute $\int_0^x t \cdot e^t \, dt$, we use integration by parts with $u = t$ and $dv = e^t \, dt$, so $du = dt$ and $v = e^t$.

Then $\int_0^x t \cdot e^t \, dt = \left. t \cdot e^t \right|_0^x - \int_0^x e^t \, dt$
$= \left. t \cdot e^t \right|_0^x - \left. e^t \right|_0^x$
$= (x \cdot e^x - 0 \cdot e^0) - (e^x - e^0)$
$= x \cdot e^x - 0 - (e^x - 1)$
$= x \cdot e^x - e^x + 1$
$= (x - 1) \cdot e^x + 1$
